query: Moscow Oil Refinery Moskovsky NPZ Kapotnya drone attacks late June and early July 2026 - has it been targeted again after June 18 2026, current operational status, and frequency of Ukrainian drone strikes on Moscow refinery
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the bottom line for your July 9–12 forecasting question:

**Has MNPZ been targeted again after June 18, 2026?** No confirmed fresh damaging strike. The two decisive attacks were June 16 and June 18, 2026. A single June 30 ZN.UA analysis references a "June 19" strike, but this is uncorroborated and low-confidence (likely referring to the June 18 overnight raid). Ukraine's late-June operations rotated to *other* refineries — Slavyansk-na-Kubani and Yaroslavl (June 28), Tyumen/Antipinsky (June 20–21), Ufa — not MNPZ.

**Current status:** Fully shut down/offline. Repair estimates range from 6 months (Reuters) to no resumption before 2027 (Frankfurter Rundschau, OilPrice), with Russian-linked sources claiming a permanently damaged cracking column.

**Strike frequency considerations relevant to July 9–12:**
- MNPZ is already 100% disabled, reducing near-term incentive to re-strike a non-operating facility.
- However, Ukraine maintains an intense, near-weekly deep-strike tempo (11 refineries in June alone) and has demonstrated repeated re-striking of persistent targets (Ryazan/Saratov ~15x each since 2022).
- Ukraine's capability to reach Kapotnya remains intact (air-defense corridor operations announced June 30).

The key tension for your forecast: Ukraine's doctrine favors re-striking facilities *when they resume operations/repairs* rather than re-hitting destroyed sites — so a July 9–12 MNPZ strike would likely depend on whether the facility shows repair/restart activity or whether Ukraine mounts another large symbolic Moscow raid.

sources.title: Reuters: Moscow Refinery unable to operate until end of year
sources.snippet: Moscow Refinery unlikely to resume before end of 2026; repairs at least six months; two processing units and storage tanks damaged.

sources.title: Moscow Refinery unlikely to resume work this year - Reuters
sources.snippet: Out of service at least six months; June 16 strike damaged unit providing more than half of capacity; shutdown after second attack June 18.

sources.title: Moscow Refinery halted - General Staff shows satellite images
sources.snippet: General Staff: suspended operations after June 16 and June 18 strikes; destroyed 30,000 m3 and 10,000 m3 reservoirs; damaged AVT-6 and KUPN units.

sources.title: Reuters: Ukrainian drones damaged key MNPZ unit
sources.snippet: June 18 damaged Euro+/AVT-6 (140,000 bpd, ~47%); June 16 forced shutdown; refinery had resumed at ~50% before second strike.

sources.title: After powerful strikes, refinery near Moscow lost all processing capacity - Reuters
sources.snippet: AVT-6 (47%) damaged June 18; AVT (53%) damaged June 16; together full capacity; processes 11 million tons/year; owned by Gazpromneft-MNPZ.

sources.title: Eight of ten major refineries disabled by Ukraine
sources.snippet: Kapotnya refinery not expected to resume production until at least 2027; 8 of 10 largest refineries disabled; June crude processing 3.95M bpd, lowest in 20+ years.

sources.title: Ukraine's Refinery Strikes Push Russia Into a Fuel Crisis
sources.snippet: Kapotnya hit early June 2026, extensive damage unlikely repaired before 2027; Putin held meeting on fuel crisis; late-June strikes on Slavyansk and Yaroslavl.

sources.title: Ukraine MoD: forces hit 11 refineries and 8 military plants in June
sources.snippet: June 2026: 11 refineries, 8 military factories, 7 fuel logistics facilities struck; Moscow (500km), Ufa, Tyumen/Antipinsky (2000km), Yaroslavl, Samara.

sources.title: From burning refineries to maritime blockade: new pressure strategy on Russia
sources.snippet: Analysis: June 19 drones hit Moscow Refinery; 50% of Russia primary refining non-functional; repair times ~6 weeks, need to exceed to force spin; June 21 Tyumen at 2000km.

sources.title: Ukraine Unmanned Systems Forces show how they breached corridor for drones to Moscow
sources.snippet: SBS breached air-defense corridor ('Polyphemus'/Ronin group) to reach Moscow; references June 6 and June 18 Kapotnya strikes; June 30, 2026.

sources.title: Kremlin shocked by strikes, no solution to fuel crisis
sources.snippet: Kapotnya halted shipments; repairs 2-3 months, one cracking column permanently damaged; full recovery ~2 years; China parts 2-year timeline.

sources.title: 24 refineries under attack: chronicle of Ukrainian campaign
sources.snippet: 2022-June 2026: 24 of 33 major refineries hit; Ryazan and Saratov hit 15 times each; repeated strikes and swarm tactics.

sources.title: Ukraine strikes two Russian oil refineries overnight, intensifying attacks
sources.snippet: June 28, 2026: Slavyansk-na-Kubani (Krasnodar) and Yaroslavl refineries struck - NOT MNPZ.

sources.title: Moscow's Biggest Oil Refinery Near Kremlin Hit Again as Drone Raid Sparks Fires
sources.snippet: Refinery hit second time in a week June 18; supplies up to 40% of Moscow fuel; 194 drones intercepted.

sources.title: Ukrainian drone strikes halted refining at Moscow's Kapotnya refinery
sources.snippet: June 18 largest attack on Moscow since war start; FP-1, Liutyi, Bober, Behemot, Morok, Bars drones; damaged ELOU-AVT-6.
